relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Peter writes to chosen sojourners scattered across the provinces.
They are held in Father, Spirit, and Messiah's covenantal cleansing.
Mercy births living hope through resurrection.
An unfading inheritance is kept for them.
Divine power guards them through trust.
Joy remains even amid temporary tests.
Tested trust becomes more precious than gold.
They love the unseen Messiah with glorified joy.
Trust receives the healing of life.
Prophets searched the grace now given.
The Spirit testified beforehand of suffering and glory.
Even messengers long to look into this good news.
Gather the mind and hope fully in coming grace.
Former desire no longer forms them.
The Holy One calls them into holy conduct.
Be holy because the Divine is holy.
Sojourn with reverent awareness before the impartial Father.
Release was not bought by perishable wealth.
Messiah's precious life releases them.
Foreknown before foundation, manifest now for them.
Through Messiah, trust and hope rest in the Divine.
Purified lives become earnest sibling-love.
They are born from imperishable living word.
Flesh and glory fade like grass.
The Lord's word remains, and this word was good-newsed to them.

contemplative_note: 1 Peter 1 is living hope in exile: mercy births, trust is refined, holiness becomes conduct, and imperishable word forms enduring love.
